steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The Mornington Railway used broad tracks, while the Rockhampton Steam Tramway used narrow gauge tracks—opposite choices within the “railway gauge muddle.”

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”By contrast” directly marks the opposition between broad and narrow gauge. The other choices all fail to capture this: “In sum” signals conclusion (wrong moment), “Similarly” claims they're alike (contradicted), and “For instance” misses the contrast. That matches choice B — B is correct.
